channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Iran praises US for cutting foreign aid funding as it looks for a Trump message on nuclear talks
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — Iran's government seems to be welcoming some recent decisions by the United States — even though they happen to come from a man Iranian operatives have allegedly been plotting to assassinate.
item.dc:creator: NASSER KARIMI and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: North Las Vegas police officer dies after shooting
item.description: A North Las Vegas police officer shot multiple times Tuesday afternoon has died from his injuries, several sources tell 8 News Now.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge to hear arguments in suit aiming to stop Trump's birthright citizenship order
item.description: A federal judge is set to hear arguments over temporarily pausing President Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship for anyone born in the U.S. to someone in the country illegally.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman And Mike Catalini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Baltimore mayor fights back against attacks on DEI initiatives with ‘Definitely Earned It’ campaign
item.description: President Donald Trump’s attack on diversity, equity and inclusion programs is leading to pushback from Baltimore's mayor.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A timeline of last week's air disaster in Washington, the deadliest in the US since 2001
item.description: Almost a week after a helicopter and airplane collision in Washington killed 67 people, investigators are still at work as salvage crews pull out chunks of aircraft.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: USAID direct hires put on leave worldwide, except those deemed essential
item.description: The Trump administration is placing U.S. Agency for International Development direct-hire staffers around the world on leave, except those deemed essential.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate confirms Pam Bondi to lead Justice Department
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday voted to confirm Pam Bondi as the next attorney general, giving her control of a Justice Department embroiled in controversy. The vote was 54-46. Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) joined all Republicans in voting in support of Bondi.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: VIDEO: 2 men facing charges following courtroom brawl in Albuquerque
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (KRQE) – A routine court hearing devolved into chaos when two people in the gallery rushed at the defendant and ended up starting a brawl. Now, those two men are facing charges. It happened at a district court hearing Friday morning for murder suspect Alexander Ortiz.
item.dc:creator: Chandler Farnsworth, KRON4

item.title: Trump picks former chief entangled in 'Sharpiegate' to lead NOAA
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has nominated Neil Jacobs to lead the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, turning to the man who was his acting NOAA chief in 2019 when Trump altered an expected hurricane impact map in what became known as the “Sharpiegate” scandal.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 arrested in connection to string of high-end burglaries, including one at Joe Burrow's home
item.description: The men have been accused of selling the stolen goods pilfered from "wealthier neighborhoods" and, according to sources, the home of Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Skincare stores close following high-pressure sales tactics probe
item.description: The owner of BH28 Skincare Consultants, Hai Baranetz, was arrested for trying to convince a 75-year-old women to take out $50,000 from her bank to become their spokesmodel.
item.dc:creator: Elise Haas, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard, RFK Jr. pass committee hurdles in win for Trump
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard and Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on Tuesday cleared crucial hurdles toward winning Senate confirmation, handing President Trump a key win as he looks for an early display of loyalty from GOP lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Video captures courtroom brawl as two men attack murder suspect during hearing
item.description: Video captured the chaotic moment two men started a brawl with murder suspect Alexander Ortiz during his hearing inside a New Mexico courtroom.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Capri Suns are now available in reusable bottles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) – Capri Sun, the beverage company famous for its juice pouches, will soon be available in reusable bottles, marking the first time the brand is expanding its single-serve offerings in two decades.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Remains of all 67 victims in deadly DC plane and chopper collision now recovered
item.description: The chief medical examiner is still trying to positively identify one set of remains, officials said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trayon White expelled from the DC Council for corruption but he's free to run for his old seat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The D.C. Council voted unanimously Tuesday to expel Councilmember Trayon White, who faces a federal trial on charges of taking tens of thousands of dollars in bribes to influence city contract decisions. White, 40, was arrested by the FBI last August.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hundreds of ducks found sick or dead along Lake Michigan in H5N1 bird flu outbreak
item.description: The outbreak started in December and has now spread from a few reports in the suburbs to hundreds in the city, along Lake Michigan, over the last week.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: White House begins migrant flights to Guantánamo Bay
item.description: The Trump administration on Tuesday began flying immigrants who entered the country illegally to Guantánamo Bay amid plans to use the facility in Cuba to detain thousands of migrants. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed the first flights from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Why it's good for NASA that an asteroid carved out 2 grand canyons on the moon
item.description: New research shows that when an asteroid slammed into the moon billions of years ago, it carved out a pair of grand canyons on the lunar far side.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Purges at FBI, DOJ trigger 'battle' for career staff
item.description: The Trump administration has removed dozens of Department of Justice (DOJ) and FBI officials and is eyeing firing perhaps thousands more in an unprecedented purge that may just be getting started.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: Live updates: Trump suggests Gaza should 'not be rebuilt' in joint presser with Netanyahu
item.description: President Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu are holding a joint press conference at the White House on Tuesday where they are discussing the future of the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, ABC 4

item.title: 5 things to know about Trump's plan to create a sovereign wealth fund
item.description: President Donald Trump is taking steps to overhaul the way the U.S. government invests its money and change the nature of America’s financial presence abroad.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: China hits US with retaliatory tariffs
item.description: China hit the U.S. back with retaliatory tariffs across the board over President Trump’s enforcement of tariffs on Chinese goods that went into effect on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: US military flying surveillance aircraft for reconnaissance missions along southern border with Mexico
item.description: The U.S. military is conducting ISR missions via aircraft along the southern border under President Donald Trump's executive order addressing border security and immigration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants rare earth elements from Ukraine in exchange for military aid
item.description: “We're looking to do a deal with Ukraine, where they're going to secure what we're giving them with their rare earth and other things,” he told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Struggling with your mental health after surviving a disaster? Here's how to look after yourself
item.description: There’s the initial shock and chaos in a collective disaster like the deadly and destructive California wildfires.
item.dc:creator: MARIAM FAM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 takeaways on Musk, Trump war with USAID
item.description: President Donald Trump and billionaire tech entrepreneur Elon Musk have moved to shut down the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), which spent some $30 billion last year on humanitarian, development and security aid to more than 100 countries.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: El Salvador offers to accept deportees from US of any nationality, including Americans: Rubio
item.description: The U.S. government cannot deport American citizens and such a move would be met with significant legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Salvage crews remove large portion of commercial jet from river after deadly air collision near DC
item.description: Salvage crews have removed a large portion of a commercial jet from the Potomac River near Washington’s Reagan National Airport on Monday, five days after a midair collision last week that killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: MLB ump fired amid gambling probe
item.description: Major League Baseball has fired an umpire as part of an investigation into sports gambling. Umpire Pat Hoberg was fired after it was revealed he shared betting accounts with a friend who bet on baseball, according to ESPN reporter Jesse Rogers and later confirmed by the league.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump signs order to create US sovereign wealth fund, eyeing TikTok purchase
item.description: President Trump on Monday directed secretaries to begin a process to create an American sovereign wealth fund, suggesting it could partially own the popular app TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, ABC 4

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Dozens of employees with the Department of Education were placed on administrative leave on Friday, allegedly after attending a diversity training course encouraged by then-Secretary Betsy DeVos during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday that after a conversation with U.S. President Donald Trump that the planned tariffs are on hold for a month, a statement confirmed by the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Stocks sink as Trump tariffs rattle Wall Street
item.description: The stock market opened with steep losses Monday in the wake of President Donald Trump's aggressive new tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese goods.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, WGN 9

item.title: Hershey isn't sure why Kisses are called 'Kisses'
item.description: Why isn't it called a Hershey’s Smooch or a Hershey’s Cone? Even the people at Hershey aren’t exactly sure.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Officials said USAID members were eventually unsuccessful and DOGE teams were able to gain access to some of the agency’s classified information, including intelligence reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Chappell Roan wins best new artist Grammy Award
item.description: Chappell Roan nudged aside Sabrina Carpenter to win the Grammy for best new artist Sunday, capping a remarkably explosive rise to fame for an artist dropped by her last record label.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5
